The largest upward price movement occurred on 2026-03-06, with a 12.21% gain closing at $90.90 on a volume of 954,254 shares, coinciding with multiple news headlines about crude oil futures and escalating Middle East supply fears, including reports of oil prices hitting a 12-month high and forecasts of WTI crude oil reaching $100 soon [1][3][7]. Another notable large up day was on 2026-03-05, with an 8.51% increase closing at $81.01 and volume of 707,030, which aligned with news of Brent crude oil prices climbing to $83 amid geopolitical tensions [16]. The largest down day was on 2026-02-02, with a 4.71% decline closing at $62.14 on volume of 414,083, though no specific driver was directly linked to this date. A high volume day on 2026-03-03 saw a 4.68% price increase closing at $74.56 with volume exceeding one million shares, suggesting strong market interest preceding the large gains in early March. Overall, the volume increased by 116.55% compared to average daily volume, reflecting heightened trading activity likely related to geopolitical and market supply concerns impacting crude oil prices during this timeframe.
